title: Han borde ta över H&M
summary_md:
# Han borde ta över H&M

**Feed**: kort-och-lang  
**Published**: 2026-06-05  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A two-host Di markets podcast centred on the AI-driven boom and a wave of new listings. Themes: cracks in AI/semiconductor names (Broadcom's weak chip guidance, Nvidia falling despite a strong report), an extraordinary IPO window (SpaceX's record valuation, Einride, Swedish serial acquirers), and Intrum's large dilutive rights issue to tackle its debt. The mood is enthusiastic about the IPO pipeline but cautious on valuations and AI concentration.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟡 SECT-B — Sectra | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Neutral | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Order intake halved but revenue/profit grew; muted 2-3% reaction; rich P/E 75-80 and weak near-term earnings; already up ~75% from bottom.

#### Reasoning

The report was volatile — order intake halved on a 12-month basis while revenue and profit rose. The muted market reaction and the ~75% recovery from the AI-selloff bottom suggest the stock has found an equilibrium. With a P/E of 75-80 and unspectacular forward earnings, there is little reason to expect a big move either way.

#### Sell Triggers

- if order intake keeps falling over coming quarters
- if forward earnings deteriorate further
- renewed AI-related sentiment selloff

> *"Så jag trodde det skulle bli större rörelse men det blev det inte."*

---

### 🟠 INTRUM — Intrum | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

7.5bn rights+directed issue to cut debt; hopes to lower financing costs by ~2bn; healthy servicing core but new owners get an unfair discount.

#### Reasoning

New CEO openly admits the debt load is too high and is raising 7.5bn to cut it, aiming to reduce rate costs by ~2bn — a strong potential return on the capital. The servicing business is a strong, low-risk brand. But hosts are unimpressed by the emission's terms: a directed issue plus guarantees hand new owners a steep discount (>400m gifted by existing holders), and ownership control is still unclear.

#### Sell Triggers

- if rights issue fails or financing costs stay near 10-11%
- if new owners dump shares after doubling money
- if private-credit crisis deepens
- unfavourable new controlling owner

> *"det känns ändå lite att de är på rätt väg, att man tar tag i sitt problem"*

---

### ⛔ AVGO — Broadcom | **AVOID** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 50%

Q3 AI-chip guidance came in below market expectations despite strong full-year outlook; shares fell ~12-13%.

#### Reasoning

Even with strong growth, big profits and above-expected full-year guidance, the third-quarter AI-chip guidance disappointed and was enough to sell the stock down sharply. Hosts argue prices, not underlying AI demand, are driving these names, and gross-margin expansion may stall — a risk to growth for richly-valued chip stocks.

#### Sell Triggers

- further guidance cuts on AI chips
- gross-margin compression as price hikes fade
- broad rotation out of AI names

> *"Och det räcker för att handla ner aktien."*

---

### 🟡 NVDA — Nvidia | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 40%

Near best-ever report and strong guidance, yet shares fell as gross-margin expectations came in slightly low.

#### Reasoning

Nvidia delivered an exceptional report with strong guidance but still fell, with gross margin slightly below expectations. Hosts read this as a sign of how stretched these AI names have become — good news no longer lifts them, signalling caution rather than outright bearishness.

#### Sell Triggers

- gross-margin disappointment
- AI capex/pricing slowdown
- continued rotation away from AI stocks

> *"Och Guidance var också starkt och föll ändå."*

---

## Summary Table

| Ticker | Company | Signal | TH | Conf | Context |
|--------|---------|--------|----|------|---------|
| **SECT-B** | Sectra | 🟡 HOLD | MT | 50% | Order intake halved but revenue/profit grew; muted 2-3% reaction; rich P/E 75-80 and weak near-term earnings; already up ~75% from bottom. |
| **INTRUM** | Intrum | 🟠 WATCH | MT | 50% | 7.5bn rights+directed issue to cut debt; hopes to lower financing costs by ~2bn; healthy servicing core but new owners get an unfair discount. |
| **AVGO** | Broadcom | ⛔ AVOID | ST | 50% | Q3 AI-chip guidance came in below market expectations despite strong full-year outlook; shares fell ~12-13%. |
| **NVDA** | Nvidia | 🟡 HOLD | ST | 40% | Near best-ever report and strong guidance, yet shares fell as gross-margin expectations came in slightly low. |
